extra.message: User-controlled search_query from request.args.get('search', '') is directly interpolated into a SQL string via an f-string: f"...LIKE '%{search_query}%'". This allows SQL injection attacks on the files table.

file.filename from user upload (multipart form) is used directly in os.path.join() and file.save() without calling secure_filename(). An attacker can send a filename like '../../../etc/malicious' to write files outside the upload directory.

The update_profile endpoint allows any authenticated user to set their own role and permissions via form fields. There is no admin check before accepting role/permissions values — any user can send role=admin and escalate privileges.

The /api/v1/update_picture endpoint fetches a URL provided directly by the user via requests.get(picture_url). This enables Server-Side Request Forgery — the attacker can make the server issue requests to internal services, cloud metadata endpoints, or arbitrary external hosts.

The DELETE /api/v1/delete-user/<user_id> endpoint has the token_required decorator commented out (lines 221-222). Any unauthenticated attacker can delete any user from the database by calling this endpoint.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The GET /api/v1/profile endpoint has the token_required decorator commented out (lines 152-153). Any unauthenticated user can retrieve any user's profile including sensitive fields like role and permissions by specifying a user_id query parameter.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The POST /api/v1/update_picture endpoint has no authentication check at all — no session check and no token_required decorator. Any unauthenticated attacker can update any user's profile picture by providing a username and picture_url.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as 'just_another_secret' in the source code. This key is used for Flask session signing and JWT token generation/verification, meaning an attacker who obtains the source can forge valid sessions and JWTs.

The custom_hash function uses MD5 (hashlib.md5) for password hashing. MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password storage. This makes all user passwords vulnerable to brute-force and rainbow table attacks.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: DEBUG is set to True enabling Flask debug mode. In production, debug mode exposes the interactive debugger, stack traces with source code, and potentially allows remote code execution via the Werkzeug debugger console.
SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY is set to False, allowing JavaScript to read session cookies via document.cookie. This amplifies the impact of any XSS vulnerability by enabling session token theft.
